Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Medal-winning Blitzboks due back in South Africa: Here’s WHEN

The Blitzboks won Team SA’s first medal at the Paris Olympics 2024 in the rugby sevens. They are due back home this week. Here’s when …

TheBlitzbokswonTeam South Africa’sfirst medalat theParis Olympics 2024in the men’s rugby sevens last week.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

The team picked up thebronze medalafter beating Australia26-19in the third-fourth playoff match.

It was thesecondtime in three Olympics that South Africa had won the bronze medal having also done so at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics in 2016.

Every member to the Blitzboks team picked upR50 000as per the financial incentives announced pre-Games by theSouth African Sports Confederation and Olympic Committee (Sascoc).

The coaches involved will also receiveR50 000.

However, as reported byThe South African website, new Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture,Gayton McKenzie, promised that he has a ‘big surprise’ for all Team SA medal winners, suggesting there could be more prize money coming their way.

Relays and team sportsGold medal: R100 000 per player and the coach R100 000Silver medal: R75 000 per player and the coach R75 000Bronze medal: R50 000 per player and the coach R50 000

Christoffel Grobbelaar, Ryan Oosthuizen, Impi Visser, Zain Davids, Quewin Nortje, Tiaan Pretorius, Shaun Williams, Selvyn Davids, Tristan Leyds, Rosko Specman, Siviwe Soyizwapi, Shilton van Wyk

Reserves:Katlego Letebele, Ronald Brown

Management:Philip Snyman, Ghafoer Luckan, Marius Schoeman, Ashley Evert

The South African men’s sevens team will arrive at Cape Town International Airport onTuesday, 30 July.

The team will arrive on Emirates flight EK 770, due to land at16:50.
